core_concepts: Class II, IIa, IIIa, IIIb, IV limits and characteristics

key_terms.definition: Visible spectrum (400-710 nm) products with emission >0.25 sec - hazard for direct long-term ocular exposure
Visible spectrum beams with total collectable radiant power ≤5 mW - includes most He-Ne lasers
UV, visible, IR devices 5-500 mW visible - ocular and skin hazards
Exceeds Class IIIb limits - hazard for scattered/diffuse reflection
Wavelength and emission duration correction factors for class limits
Energy measured through 7mm aperture with 10^-3 steradian acceptance

regulatory_references: 21 CFR 1040.10(d)(Tables I, II-A, II, III-A, III-B) - Class limit tables
21 CFR 1040.10(d)(Tables IV, V) - k1 and k2 values
learning_objectives: Differentiate between all five laser product classes
Understand wavelength and duration factors in classification
Identify hazard levels for each class
Apply class limit tables to determine product classification
